description: Event-Sourced Engineering Memory. Every significant engineering event MUST conform to this structure. Low-quality events = garbage memory = AI hallucination.

field_definitions.type: BUG_FIX | FEATURE | SCHEMA | REFACTOR | CONFIG | ARCH_DECISION | INCIDENT

severity.description: Failure memory weight — AI weights reasoning toward high-severity zones

CRITICAL.trigger: Financial inconsistency, data loss, auth bypass, production down

HIGH.trigger: Data encoding corruption, DB schema mismatch, API contract break, feature broken for all users

MEDIUM.trigger: Runtime crash, proxy failure, feature broken for some users

INFO.trigger: Docs update, ADR record, refactor with no behavior change

field_definitions.why: ROOT CAUSE narrative — WHY this happened, not just WHAT. This is the most important field. Bad example: 'bug in code'. Good example: 'Node.js >=17 changed DNS resolution to prefer IPv6, but backend bound to IPv4 only'.
field_definitions.root_cause: One-sentence technical summary of the root cause (for indexing/search)
field_definitions.solution: What was changed to fix it — specific and actionable

blast_radius.description: What other parts of the system could be affected. Used for impact analysis.

field_definitions.affected_nodes: Graph node IDs (FunctionNode, ClassNode, APINode, DBTableNode names)
field_definitions.related_adr: Array of ADR filenames. Links decision records to incidents.
field_definitions.caused_by: Array of event_ids that triggered or contributed to this event (causal chain)
field_definitions.related_incidents: Array of event_ids in the same failure cluster
field_definitions.data_loss: boolean — true if event caused any data deletion or corruption
field_definitions.prevention: How to prevent this from happening again — actionable rule
field_definitions.rollback_strategy: Mandatory if affecting finance, inventory, or orders domains. How to revert the system state if this fix fails in production.
field_definitions.governance_approval: boolean — true if the task passed strict governance checks (mandatory for critical domains).
field_definitions.git_commit: Short git hash at time of fix. 'no-git' if not tracked.

GOOD_EVENT: why field explains the non-obvious root cause
prevention has a concrete actionable rule
affected_files are specific paths, not vague descriptions
BAD_EVENT_ANTI_PATTERNS: title: 'fix bug' — too vague, not searchable
why: 'there was an error' — no information
prevention: missing — means we'll repeat this
severity: CRITICAL for every event — cry wolf, loses signal
